items.title: Lithium giant offers free car wash for residents battling dust from its mine

children.content: Residents in the South West town of Greenbushes, plagued by dust from Australia's oldest and largest lithium mine, say a plan for a free car wash by the mine's owner misses the mark.

gallery.altText: An aerial shot of the Greenbushes mining pits.
Composite image of rock dust on the fronds of a palm tree and obscuring a car windshield.
A photo of Greenbushes resident Rick Fryer sitting at home.

thumbnail.altText: An aerial shot of the Greenbushes mining pits.
Composite image of rock dust on the fronds of a palm tree and obscuring a car windshield.
A photo of Greenbushes resident Rick Fryer sitting at home.

items.title: Residents worried about emergency access after bridge closes

children.content: The closure of a bridge linking Wheatbelt residents to their town sparks concern about emergency response times and access to services, including disability support.

image.altText: A woman wearing glasses and a beaning looks at the camera.

items.title: Government secures aircraft to fly Hantavirus cruise passengers to Perth

children.content: A suitable aircraft to bring home passengers from the Hantavirus-hit cruise has been secured and will repatriate the group of six later this week.

image.altText: man in blue suit and green tie with still face looking straight ahead

items.title: Record native title compensation decision hailed as a 'watershed case'

children.content: Andrew Forrest's Fortescue has been ordered to hand over the largest native title compensation payout in Australian history. But some say it falls painfully short of what is owed.

articles.title: Record payout after billionaire built mine without traditional owners' permission

children.content: Andrew Forrest's Fortescue has been ordered to pay $150 million to WA traditional owners after mining their land without permission.

image.altText: A composite image of Andrew Forrest (left) and Michael Woodley situated at a Fortescue mine in WA.

gallery.altText: Michael Woodley speaks to reporters outside court.

thumbnail.altText: Michael Woodley speaks to reporters outside court.

items.title: Man facing drug charges after woman's body found outside cafe in Joondalup

children.content: Police are investigating after a woman's body was discovered on Grand Boulevard in Joondalup, with a 35-year-old man charged with a string of drug offences.

image.altText: A note on the cafe window where the woman's body was found.

items.title: Inquest finds treatment of prisoner in lead up to death 'reprehensible'

children.content: Corey Desmond Devree died when he was left alone for extended periods of time at the prison's paint workshop.

image.altText: The front gate of a medium security prison.

items.title: Ex-bikie boss who paid sniper to kill rival gets 34 years in jail

children.content: David Pye, who was convicted last year of hiring a sniper to assassinate his rival Nick Martin during a drag racing event, was sentenced to 34 years in jail.

gallery.altText: A man named David Pye has tattoos and smiles as he walks down stairs.

thumbnail.altText: A man named David Pye has tattoos and smiles as he walks down stairs.

items.title: Australians 'literally dying' while they wait months for aged care packages

children.content: Opposition MPs accuse the federal government of burying figures showing Australians are waiting a year for residential aged care and at-home support packages.

image.altText: A bed in a resident's room in an aged care home.

items.title: Investors, renters and home owners react to housing reforms

children.content: Housing policy changes announced in the federal budget could make it easier for some to buy homes, but others are planning to sell before they lose too much.

image.altText: A woman sits with her legs crossed on a stone wall overlooking the ocean.

items.title: Terror accused charged with child abuse, extremist material offences

children.content: A man accused of planning terrorist attacks in Perth is charged with further offences, including possession of child abuse material and violent extremist material on his devices.

image.altText: A man smiles at a camera with a digger behind him

items.title: Homebuilding reforms to let WA customers walk away from failing builders sooner

children.content: New legislation introduced to state parliament aim to prevent Western Australians from having to suffer the financial and emotional strain of not having a home if their building company collapses.

image.altText: A couple inspects their unfinished Nicheliving home.

items.title: Sexual abuse, a thwarted Olympic dream, and the sister who uncovered the truth

children.content: Ian Walker was one of the most promising athletes in the country until his life started to unravel, and the search for the truth in his tragic story has finally come to light.

gallery.altText: An older image of three young siblings arm in arm smiling at each other.
A young man wearing black pants and a blue jumper stands smiling beneath a poster of Jim Morrison.

thumbnail.altText: An older image of three young siblings arm in arm smiling at each other.
A young man wearing black pants and a blue jumper stands smiling beneath a poster of Jim Morrison.

items.title: Angus Taylor proposes one-for-one migration cap tied to housing

children.content: The Coalition wants to limit Australia's net migration intake to only one person per new home built, but would wait until in government to decide what visa types to slash if elected.

image.altText: A man in a suit extends his hand in exclamation while speaking to a group of journalists.

items.title: E-scooter rider dies after being hit by car while crossing Perth highway

children.content: A man in his 40s is struck by a hatchback and killed while riding an e-scooter across Reid Highway in Perth's northern suburbs.

image.altText: A generic photograph of an unidentified WA Police officer wearing a high visibility police vest over a blue uniform.

items.title: Women's refuge turns to smarter solutions to fight tracking technology

children.content: A women's shelter in regional Western Australia has had to adapt to an increase in technology-facilitated violence to keep victims safe.

image.altText: Woman holding an iphone

items.title: Voss exit shuts door on Carlton's premiership era that never was

children.content: Michael Voss leaves Carlton having taken the club to places it has rarely been in a quarter of a century, but still adrift from where it ultimately wants to be.

image.altText: Michael Voss with both fists raised in celebration

items.title: Solar eclipse to attract thousands to 'middle of nowhere' in 2028

children.content: One of the longest solar eclipses this century, albeit for five minutes, is set to put the remote East Kimberley on the world map.

gallery.altText: A child wearing eclipse glasses looks up at the sky
blonde woman with blue top posing next to an information sign

thumbnail.altText: A child wearing eclipse glasses looks up at the sky
Artemis II — a sunrise for orion
blonde woman with blue top posing next to an information sign

items.title: Which country has won Eurovision the most? Take our More or Less quiz

children.content: We've put down our tiny flags and picked up our record books to challenge what you think you might know about Eurovision.

image.altText: Kate Miller-Heidke is reaching towards the camera, suspended on a pole, with a microphone in opposite hand.

items.title: Blockbuster movies are connecting new audiences to classical music

children.content: From 19th-century concert traditions to Hollywood’s Golden Age, and back to the concert hall, movies are helping audiences experience live orchestral music.

image.altText: animation of young boy with brown hair with black dragon

items.title: The rising populist tide is making Labor bolder

children.content: If this week's budget marked the start of the Albanese government's campaign for a third term in office, tonight's budget reply marks Angus Taylor's pitch to prevent a Liberal wipe-out.

image.altText: Anthony Albanese wears a suit and blue tie, and black-rimmed glasses. He stares intently just past the camera.

avatar.alt: David Speers sits on a couch in front of a yellow screen, smiling at the camera, in a suit and open-necked shirt.

items.title: Economists divided on extent of property price hit from budget tax overhaul

children.content: Some economists are forecasting price falls of up to 5 per cent as a result of measures to reduce intergenerational housing inequality.

image.altText: A house is advertised for auction in the south-west Sydney suburb of Punchbowl.

items.title: Father allegedly high on 'nangs' in crash that killed daughters, as scuffle erupts outside court

children.content: A Perth father was allegedly high on 'nangs' during a crash that killed his two daughters, as a scuffle with media erupts at court.

gallery.altText: A wide shot of the front facade and entrance to the Midland Courthouse.
A section of road with some trees alongside.

thumbnail.altText: A wide shot of the front facade and entrance to the Midland Courthouse.
A section of road with some trees alongside.

items.title: Former Australian cricketer named first foreign English selector

children.content: The appointment of its first Australian selector, its first female coach and a new-look team has made it a landmark day for England's men's cricket team as it prepares to plunge into its post-Ashes Test recovery.

image.altText: Batter raising his creicket bat to acknowledge the crowd

items.title: Canvas developers could be added to 'sucker list' after deal with hackers

children.content: The company that runs Canvas has painted a target on its back for future extortion attempts by making a deal with hackers, according to cybersecurity experts.

image.altText: a man sitting at a computer

items.title: Oldest evidence of dentistry found in 59,000-year-old Neanderthal tooth

children.content: Archaeologists have found a hole in a 59,000-year-old tooth, which they say was drilled to treat a painful cavity. The find suggests Neanderthals could perform complex medical procedures.

image.altText: Museum diorama of Neanderthal family in a cave.

items.title: 'Portrait of anger' by Australian cartoonist wins $60,000 Stella Prize

children.content: Lee Lai's debut graphic novel, Stone Fruit, was longlisted for the Stella Prize in 2022. Four years on, she's won the prestigious award for her latest book, Cannon.

image.altText: A close-up photo of a person with short dark hair and glasses, smiling and wearing a tan checked shirt.

items.title: Murdoch Uni admits accessibility needs fix after student's viral video

children.content: The former Murdoch University student's video highlights the difficulty wheelchair users have navigating the campus since stair lifts were removed in 2023.

gallery.altText: Young woman with curly brown hair sitting in wheelchair
Students walk past Murdoch University's library at dusk.

thumbnail.altText: Young woman with curly brown hair sitting in wheelchair
Students walk past Murdoch University's library at dusk.
